topic.name: Financial Oversight and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and oversight within financial institutions and federal agencies.

Document 1: Promoting Transparency in Financial Reporting Act of 2007
Requires the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to provide oral testimony
Document 2: Directs the Comptroller General to complete an audit of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
a detailed report to Congress
Document 3: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968
Prohibits such incumbent from serving as Director after September 4, 2013

topic.name: Medicaid and Medicare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to Medicaid and Medicare programs, specifically addressing fraud prevention and coverage enhancements.

Document 1: expands the authority of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) to exclude from participation in federal health programs
requires Medicare Advantage organizations to report instances of fraud or abuse to CMS within 60 days
Document 2: allows medical assistance under the Medicaid program to include payment for inpatient, nonhospital, residential substance use disorder treatment for individuals over age 21
Document 3: amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to alter coverage requirements under a state plan for medical assistance
shall also provide biennial medical assistance to the individual for one cranial prosthesis

topic.name: Education Legislation and Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at modifying federal student aid and educational program eligibility to enhance access and support for diverse educational opportunities.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand the programs eligible to participate in federal student aid

makes eligible for Direct Loan program and Federal Perkins Loan program participation an educational program that provides at least 250 hours of instruction over a minimum of 5 weeks that leads to an industry-recognized credential.
Document 2: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to limit waiver authority of the Department of Education
prohibits ED from waiving statutory provisions that ban Pell Grant eligibility and federal financial aid for incarcerated students.

topic.name: Tariff Suspensions on Goods
topic.description: The documents discuss temporary suspensions of duties on specific goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on reusable surgical wrappers of textile materials.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 2: extend the temporary suspension of duty on filament tow of rayon.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures containing Thiencarbazone-methyl, Isoxadifen-ethyl, and Tembotrione.
Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States

topic.name: Veterans Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving healthcare services and support for veterans in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits recovery by the United States of medical costs for service-connected disabilities
Addresses healthcare provisions for veterans' non-service-connected disabilities.
Document 2: Authorizes land conveyance for a veterans' medical facility
Supports construction of healthcare infrastructure for veterans.
Document 3: Establishes an Office of the Ombudsman in the Veterans Health Administration
Aims to address veterans' healthcare complaints and issues.

topic.name: Environmental Regulation and Fees
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to environmental regulations, fees, and incentives associated with greenhouse gas emissions and offshore oil and gas activities.

Document 1: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions emitted directly by livestock
carrying out any Act or program to reduce the effects of greenhouse gas emissions on climate change.
Document 2: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of Energy to establish a fee for any nonproducing oil or gas leases
on outer Continental Shelf land in the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations that require each Bureau of Ocean Energy Management, Regulation and Enforcement (BOEMRE) employee to be subject to random testing
Prohibits an individual from being employed by any person conducting any activity under a lease or permit issued by BOEMRE.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration and Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focusing on border security and the protection of vulnerable individuals, particularly in the context of immigration and human rights.

Document 1: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
require the Secretary to complete construction of such fencing
Document 2: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
require the Secretary to complete construction of such fencing
Document 3: impose a fine or five-year prison term
for the purposes of female genital mutilation

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons Oversight
topic.description: The documents focus on the assessment and oversight of foreign countries' electromagnetic pulse weapons programs by the Director of National Intelligence.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Land Transfer Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative actions related to the transfer of land ownership and designations involving various parcels of land.

Document 1: transfers, upon the request of the city of Flagstaff, Arizona, and the Burlington Northern and Santa Fe Railway Company, to a specified parcel of land
any reversionary interest of the United States in and to a specified parcel of land
Document 2: amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to redesignate the Olympic Wilderness
Daniel J. Evans Wilderness
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey the Forest Service Lake Hill Administrative Site in Summit County, Colorado

topic.name: Legislation on Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at protecting and managing the welfare of vulnerable groups, including unregistered sex offenders and unaccompanied alien children.

Document 1: Finding Fugitive Sex Offenders Act of 2011
authorize the United States Marshals Service to issue administrative subpoenas solely for the investigation of unregistered sex offenders.
Document 2: Our Communities, Our Choices Act of 2015
require consultation with state and local elected officials and a public hearing before awarding grants or contracts to provide non-federal housing facilities for unaccompanied alien children.
Document 3: UAC State Notification Act of 2014
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to notify the governor of a state within 48 hours before the placement of an unaccompanied alien child.

topic.name: Youth Mental Health and Autism Research
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at improving research, intervention, and support services for youth mental health and autism spectrum disorders.

Document 1: establish a Youth Mental Health Research Network
conduct or support of youth mental health research and intervention services
Document 2: reauthorize appropriations for programs established under the Combating Autism Act
autism education, early detection, and intervention program
Document 3: provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals with mental illness
comprehensive community-based mental health systems

topic.name: Pharmaceutical and Drug Policy Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at regulating the use, distribution, and monitoring of pharmaceuticals and controlled substances.

Document 1: requires a state that receives a prescription drug monitoring program implementation and enhancement grant to set aside at least 20% of grant funds
facilitate interstate electronic information sharing about prescriptions for controlled substances
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act
shall not apply to any person acting in compliance with state laws relating to the production, possession, distribution, dispensation, administration, or delivery of marijuana
Document 3: amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act
require the Food and Drug Administration to facilitate the development and expedite the review of a new drug, biological product, or device

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: Legislation focused on the planning, design, and construction of water recycling and rights settlement projects in collaboration with local entities.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with Pima County, Arizona, to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities
enhance and restore riparian habitat
Document 2: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement
Directs the Secretary of Interior to execute both agreements
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with the city of San Juan Capistrano, California, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility
participate in the design, planning, and construction of a project to expand reclaimed water distribution

topic.name: Homeowner Financial Assistance Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing financial assistance and tax benefits to homeowners, particularly in the context of foreclosure mitigation and home purchasing incentives.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to provide assistance in specified amounts to the National Foreclosure Mitigation Counseling Program
Directs the NRC to establish a procedure by which the Program shall direct funds to such agencies.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase by 25% the allowable tax deduction for mortgage interest
waive the requirement to repay credit amounts under the first-time homebuyer tax credit.

Document 3: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act to revise the limitation on the Secretary of the Treasury's authority to purchase troubled assets through the Troubled Assets Relief Program.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection, restoration, and sustainable management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.
Document 2: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects
The bill establishes the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to coordinate federal actions that are designed to preserve and protect coral reef ecosystems.
Document 3: This bill amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act to authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force
Priority is given to establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Nutrition
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agricultural practices, crop insurance, and nutritional programs aimed at improving child health and food assistance.

Document 1: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 to end agricultural direct payments
beginning with the 2013 crop year.
Document 2: Enhancing Child Health with Automatic School Meal Enrollment Act of 2009
direct the Secretary of Agriculture to make performance awards to states that demonstrate outstanding performance.
Document 3: Protect our Prairies Act - Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act
to reduce crop insurance assistance and noninsured crop disaster assistance for crops grown on native sod acreage.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting and regulating the use of renewable energy sources and technologies in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program.
Requires renewable fuel to be advanced biofuel.
Document 2: Amends the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978 to allow state control over renewable energy rates.
Allows facilities generating electric energy from renewable sources to sell energy at specified rates.
Document 3: Amends the National Energy Conservation Policy Act to support alternative fueled vehicles.
Authorizes federal agencies to participate in programs for renewable energy infrastructure.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of personally identifiable information and the requirements for reporting breaches and access to government reports.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to notify individuals of breaches of personally identifiable information.
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress annually on breaches and security measures.
Document 2: Requires the Government Publishing Office (GPO) to provide free electronic access to congressionally mandated reports.
Agencies must redact any information that may not be publicly released under the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA).
Document 3: Makes it unlawful for federal employees to willfully disclose protected health information.
Liability for individuals who aid in unauthorized disclosures of personally identifiable information.

topic.name: Accountability in Taxation and Fraud
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing accountability and addressing fraud within the tax system and among public officials.

Document 1: Justice for Victims of IRS Scams and Identity Theft Act of 2016
expedite the review of identity theft cases involving IRS impersonation
Document 2: Public Official Accountability Act
two-year increase in prison sentences for public officials convicted of crimes during official duty
Document 3: Tax Return Preparer Accountability Act of 2015
requires regulation of tax return preparers and penalties for non-compliance

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating financial institutions and ensuring proper oversight of financial practices.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to make the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) a 'covered agency'
Prohibits information submitted to the CFPB in the course of its supervisory or regulatory process from being construed as waiving...
Document 2: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to make the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) a 'covered agency'
Prohibits information submitted to the CFPB in the course of its supervisory or regulatory process from being construed as waiving...
Document 3: Prohibits Members of Congress, senior congressional staffers, and administration executives from purchasing or selling registered securities...
Subjects any person who violates such requirement to specified federal administrative penalties relating to material nonpublic information...

topic.name: Migratory Bird Conservation and Regulation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection, regulation, and conservation of migratory birds and related activities.

Document 1: Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to permit the taking of any migratory game bird
contains a standing crop (including an aquatic crop)
Document 2: Amends the Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamp Act
allows the Department of the Interior every five years to set the cost for Federal Migratory Bird Hunting and Conservation Stamps
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to make it unlawful to knowingly sponsor or exhibit an animal in an animal fighting venture
Prohibits knowingly sponsoring or exhibiting a bird in a fighting venture

topic.name: Legislative Actions and Designations
topic.description: This topic encompasses the designation of facilities and legislative measures related to public services and federal land management in the United States.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4500 Sunny Isle Shopping Center in Christiansted, St. Croix, United States Virgin Islands, as the 'Florence Louise Thomas Post Office.'
Document 2: This bill authorizes the Department of the Interior and the Department of Agriculture to place on federal land at points along the American Discovery Trail acceptable signage donated to the United States for that purpose.

topic.name: Transportation Regulation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to the regulation of transportation systems, including rail and drone operations.

Document 1: Bakken crude oil to be transported by rail
maximum volatility set by the New York Mercantile Exchange
Document 2: prohibit the Secretary of Transportation from authorizing unmanned aircraft systems
operate an unmanned aircraft system as a weapon
Document 3: Secretary of Transportation to assess a civil penalty
shift change of rail carrier employees causes a blockage

topic.name: Forensic Science and STEM Education
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at enhancing forensic science research and promoting STEM education for underrepresented groups.

Document 1: Establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences.
Directs NSF to create a merit-reviewed, competitive forensic science research grants program to improve the foundation and practice of forensic science.
Document 2: Authorize the Department of Education (ED) to provide grants to local educational agencies to encourage the ongoing development of programs and curricula for girls and underrepresented minorities in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM).
Document 3: Requires the Director of the National Science Foundation (NSF) to establish a nanotechnology in the schools program awarding matching grants to certain eligible institutions.
Provide nanotechnology education to students and teachers.

topic.name: Tax-advantaged Savings Accounts
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures aimed at establishing or modifying tax-advantaged savings accounts for retirement, education, and health-related expenses.

Document 1: establish a new retirement option for all employees
The American Savings Account Fund is tax-exempt
Document 2: allow tax-free rollovers of amounts in qualified tuition programs
designed to enable individuals with disabilities to save
Document 3: Repeals provisions that limit payments from health and medical savings accounts
increase taxes on distributions from health and medical savings accounts

topic.name: Support for Business Development
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at facilitating business growth and ownership, particularly for women and private entities in specific sectors.

Document 1: amends the Water Resources Development Act to expedite permit evaluations for private entities
authorizes the Secretary of the Army to accept and expend funds contributed by a private entity
Document 2: establishes the Commission on Women's Business Ownership
reviews the status of women business owners across the United States
Document 3: amends the Small Business Act to revise criteria for women's business centers
includes the ability to provide training to socially and economically disadvantaged unmarried women

topic.name: Tribal Sovereignty and Education
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legal rights of federally recognized tribes in the U.S. to access federal services, manage land leases, and promote Native American language education.

Document 1: U.S. citizen of Pascua Yaqui blood eligible for federal services
benefits made available to members of federally recognized tribes.
Document 2: Authorizes the Coquille Indian Tribe to obtain 99-year lease authority for trust land.
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act to authorize grants for Native American language programs
support the use by schools of Native American languages as their primary language of instruction.

topic.name: Senior and Military Benefits Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on benefits and economic adjustments for seniors and military personnel in the United States.

Document 1: Consumer Price Index for Seniors (CPI-S)
monthly changes in expenditures for consumption that are typical for individuals 62 years of age or older.
Document 2: Social Security Totalization Agreement Reform Act
amends title II of the Social Security Act
Document 3: Military Pay Improvement Act
waives the basic military pay adjustment tied to increases in the Employment Cost Index.

topic.name: Regulatory Reform Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving regulatory processes and reducing bureaucratic burdens across various governmental agencies.

Document 1: Department of Labor as a 'covered agency' for regulatory flexibility analysis provisions.
Document 2: require each agency to publish a biennial agricultural regulatory flexibility agenda
authorizes an agricultural entity that is adversely affected by a final agency action to seek judicial review.
Document 3: Establishes the Office of Regulatory Analysis in the executive branch as an independent establishment.
Requires Congress to pass an annual bill establishing a National Regulatory Budget that sets an overall regulatory cost cap.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy on Iran
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislative and diplomatic efforts to manage relations and sanctions related to Iran.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of State, in conjunction with the Secretary of the Treasury, the Secretary of Commerce, the Secretary of Defense, and the intelligence community, to conduct annual assessments of the effectiveness of sanctions against Iran.
assessments of the effectiveness of sanctions against Iran.
Document 2: Directs the President to appoint a high-level U.S. representative or special envoy who shall: (1) seek to ease tensions and normalize relations between the United States and Iran.
lead U.S. diplomatic efforts with regard to Iran.

topic.name: Support for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing assistance and services to vulnerable groups such as veterans and homeless individuals.

Document 1: expands access to Department of Defense (DOD) homeowners assistance for qualified members of the Armed Forces
consult with the Secretaries of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) and the Treasury regarding the condition of housing markets
Document 2: allows homeless veterans' comprehensive services grant funds to be used to provide funding for service center staff
direct the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to annually increase the rate of such payment to reflect anticipated changes in the cost of furnishing services
Document 3: deem a student independent for financial aid purposes if such student has been verified as both a homeless child or youth
verification to take place during the school year in which the application for such aid is submitted

topic.name: Public Health and Awareness Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving public health through awareness campaigns, funding requirements, and research initiatives.

Document 1: Prohibits a U.S. government grant, contract, or cooperative agreement for the reconstruction of Iraq to be awarded unless the government of Iraq provides at least 50% matching funds from non-U.S. government contributions.
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services...to develop a national Shaken Baby Syndrome public health campaign.
Directs the Secretary to carry out communication, education, and training about Shaken Baby Syndrome prevention.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services...to expand and intensify research to develop advanced imaging technologies for prostate cancer detection.
Directs the Secretary to carry out a national campaign to increase awareness and knowledge with respect to the need for prostate cancer screening.

topic.name: Consumer Financial Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing consumer rights and protections in financial transactions and credit reporting.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act
restrict the award of court costs and attorney fees
Document 2: failure by a financial institution or any of its directors, officers, employees, or agents to report a suspicious transaction
good faith effort to follow existing policies, procedures, and processes
Document 3: Amends the Fair Credit Reporting Act
require certain consumer reporting agencies to disclose, without charge, a current credit score

topic.name: Spectrum Management Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, efficiency, and commercialization of electromagnetic spectrum use.

Document 1: requires the National Telecommunications and Information Administration to conduct prize competitions to accelerate the development and commercialization of technology that improves spectrum efficiency
the Federal Communications Commission must publish a technical paper providing criteria for the design of such competitions
Document 2: amends the National Telecommunications and Information Administration Organization Act to make amounts available from the Spectrum Relocation Fund
to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of their use of electromagnetic spectrum under government station licenses
Document 3: establish the First Responders Interoperable Device Availability Trust Fund

requires that amounts from the Fund be used by the Administrator of the National Telecommunications and Information Administration to make grants, beginning in FY2012, to purchase interoperable devices for use by public safety personnel

topic.name: Veterans and Reserve Benefits
topic.description: Legislation focused on the rights, benefits, and support for members of the National Guard, reserves, and their families.

Document 1: enrollment of members of the National Guard and reserve in health and dental care programs for veterans
education and outreach about the VA programs, benefits, and services
Document 2: reduce the minimum age at which a member of the reserves may retire
days of active duty or active service used to reduce retirement age
Document 3: death gratuity for a member of the Armed Forces who dies while serving
payable to parent, brother, or sister as designated by the member

topic.name: Lead Exposure Mitigation
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at reducing lead exposure in housing and drinking water, particularly for vulnerable populations such as children and veterans.

Document 1: Amends the Residential Lead-Based Paint Hazard Reduction Act of 1992
set the environmental intervention blood lead level for a confirmed concentration of lead
Document 2: amends the Safe Drinking Water Act
requiring the Environmental Protection Agency to publish revised guidance for school officials about reducing exposure to lead from drinking water

topic.name: Labor Rights and Education
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing labor rights and educational opportunities in various sectors, particularly in healthcare and higher education.

Document 1: establish a partnership grant program to award matching grants to eligible entities to carry out comprehensive programs to provide education to nurses
create a pipeline to nursing for incumbent ancillary health care workers
Document 2: ensure that bid specifications, project agreements, or other controlling documents do not require or prohibit a bidder from entering into agreements with a labor organization
discriminate against such a party because it did or did not become a signatory
Document 3: provide collective bargaining rights for students enrolled at a private institution of higher education
perform work for remuneration at the institution's direction

topic.name: Legal Protections and Rights in Detention
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at ensuring humane treatment and legal rights for individuals, particularly children and detainees, in the context of immigration and military detention.

Document 1: apprehension procedures for immigration enforcement-related activities
ensure that child custody and family interests can be considered in any immigration detention action
Document 2: prohibit any person in the custody or control of the United States from being subject to any treatment or technique of interrogation not authorized
makes such prohibition inapplicable with respect to any person in the custody or control of the United States pursuant to a U.S. criminal or immigration law
Document 3: repeals provisions of the Military Commissions Act of 2006 that eliminated the jurisdiction of any court to hear or consider applications for a writ of habeas corpus
restoring habeas corpus rights existing prior to the enactment of such Act

topic.name: Early Childhood and Educational Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing early childhood education and support systems for students and educators, particularly focusing on funding, training, and mentoring programs.

Document 1: Requires states to issue guidance to local educational agencies regarding permissible uses of school improvement funds to serve preschool children.
Directs the Secretary of Education to issue guidance to LEAs on the use of funds for prekindergarten programs.
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to improve training for principals.
Establish learning communities within schools to analyze and improve teaching and learning.
Document 3: Establishes a transition-to-success mentoring program to assist at-risk students in transitioning from middle to high school.
Requires grantees to assign each at-risk student a success coach to create a plan of success.

topic.name: Voting and Administrative Services Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to voting regulations and the provision of administrative services in governmental operations.

Document 1: Requires federal agencies to participate in the International Cooperative Administrative Support Services system for overseas operations.
Mandates detailed explanations for opting out of the system.
Document 2: Amends the National Voter Registration Act to address noncitizen voting.
Requires posting notices at polling places regarding voting eligibility.
Document 3: Directs reimbursement for states establishing online voter registration programs.
Focuses on empowering voter registration through technology.

topic.name: Animal Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures aimed at protecting animal welfare and regulating the use of animals in specific contexts.

Document 1: prohibit the use or possession of body-gripping traps
civil fine of up to $500 for each body-gripping trap possessed or used
Document 2: unlawful for a licensed firearms dealer to authorize an employee to possess or transfer a firearm
civil and criminal penalties for knowingly violating the requirements of this Act
Document 3: impose a fine and/or prison term for possessing, shipping, transporting, purchasing, selling, delivering, or receiving any horse
reduces the prison term to one year if the offense involves less than five horses

topic.name: Aviation Security Regulations
topic.description: The documents discuss regulations and requirements related to enhancing security measures in commercial aviation, particularly focusing on cockpit access and covert testing of security systems.

Document 1: installation of secondary barriers to prevent access to the flight deck
such barriers remain locked while the aircraft is in flight
Document 2: installation of secondary barriers, other than the cockpit door
such barriers remain locked while the aircraft is in flight
Document 3: covert test of a transportation security system
requires the head of each covert testing office to make sure that a covert testing person or group is accompanied by a cover team

topic.name: Food Labeling and Assistance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on food labeling requirements and assistance programs aimed at improving food access and nutrition.

Document 1: Amends the Emergency Food Assistance Act of 1983 to provide for the increased purchase of Kosher and Halal food
modify the labeling of the commodities list under the emergency food assistance program
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to establish the local food for healthy families program
provide low-income families with incentives to purchase fruits and vegetables
Document 3: requires the market name of genetically modified salmon to include 'Genetically Engineered' or 'GE'
the FDA must conduct another environmental assessment of the salmon

topic.name: Recognition of Native Communities
topic.description: Legislation aimed at recognizing and compensating unrecognized Native communities in Southeast Alaska.

Document 1: Unrecognized Southeast Alaska Native Communities Recognition and Compensation Act
permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages of Haines, Ketchikan, Petersburg, Tenakee, and Wrangell, Alaska, to organize as Urban Corporations
Document 2: Unrecognized Southeast Alaska Native Communities Recognition and Compensation Act
permit the Native residents of each of the Native Villages of Haines, Ketchikan, Petersburg, Tenakee, and Wrangell, Alaska, to organize as Urban Corporations
Document 3: Amends the Native American Programs Act of 1974
promote the economic and social self-sufficiency of Native Americans

topic.name: Legal Protections for Servicemembers
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at safeguarding the rights and welfare of servicemembers, particularly in legal contexts such as custody and arbitration.

Document 1: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to make Public Company Accounting Oversight Board disciplinary proceedings public
Document 2: Amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act to provide that if a court renders a temporary order for custodial responsibility for a child based solely on a deployment or anticipated deployment of a parent-servicemember
Prohibits a servicemember's deployment or possible deployment from being considered as the sole factor in the determination of a child's best interest
Document 3: This bill amends the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA) to make any predispute arbitration agreement invalid or unenforceable against a servicemember
A servicemember's waiver of any SCRA rights and protections shall be effective only if made after a specific dispute has arisen and the dispute is identified in the waiver.

topic.name: Postal Service and Customer Service Improvement
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing service standards and customer relations within the U.S. Postal Service and federal agencies.

Document 1: Repeals the service standards for market-dominant products implemented by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) on January 5, 2015
directs USPS to reinstate the service standards that were in effect on December 31, 2011.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to develop performance measures to determine whether federal agencies are providing high-quality customer service
Requires each federal agency to collect information from its customers regarding the quality of its customer services.

topic.name: Law Enforcement and Cybercrime Prevention
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing law enforcement capabilities in rural areas and addressing cybercrime awareness and prevention, particularly for vulnerable populations.

Document 1: authorizes appropriations for rural drug enforcement assistance
training rural law enforcement officers in the investigation of drug trafficking
Document 2: make two-year grants to educational agencies for Internet crime awareness
contract for research studies on Internet crime awareness and prevention
Document 3: emergency appropriations for state and local law enforcement assistance
Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant program

topic.name: Taxpayer Funding and Family Planning
topic.description: Legislation regarding the prohibition of federal funding for population control and family planning activities, including abortion procedures.

Document 1: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program
including any abortion procedure.
Document 2: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program
including any abortion procedure.
Document 3: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program
including any abortion procedure.

topic.name: National Nurse for Public Health
topic.description: Legislation establishing the role and responsibilities of the National Nurse for Public Health within the Office of the Surgeon General to enhance public health initiatives.

Document 1: establish the position of National Nurse for Public Health within the Office of the Surgeon General
providing leadership and coordination of Public Health Service nursing professional affairs
Document 2: require the Department of Health and Human Services to designate the Chief Nurse Officer as the National Nurse for Public Health
conducting outreach and education
Document 3: establish the position of National Nurse for Public Health within the Office of the Surgeon General
promote the dissemination of evidence-based practice in educating the public on health promotion and disease prevention activities

topic.name: Military Justice Reforms
topic.description: Legislative changes aimed at improving the military justice system and addressing issues related to sex offender registration and financial restitution.

Document 1: Amends the Sex Offender Registration and Notification Act to direct the Department of Defense to provide to the Attorney General information required under such Act

regarding persons who are: (1) released from military corrections facilities or convicted if the sentences adjudged by courts-martial under the Uniform Code of Military Justice do not include confinement
Document 2: Amends the Uniform Code of Military Justice to require, when a court-martial sentence that resulted in forfeiture of pay and allowances is later set aside or disapproved
that the restoration of the forfeited amounts be adjusted for inflation
Document 3: Requires the payment of compound interest by the United States on the payment of any claim in connection with the correction of a military record
that involves the setting aside of a conviction by court-martial

topic.name: District of Columbia Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the governance and military support of the District of Columbia.

Document 1: Cedes the District of Columbia to Maryland after Maryland's acceptance of such retrocession.
Maintains the exclusive legislative authority and control of Congress over the Area.
Document 2: Authorizes the commanding general of the District of Columbia National Guard to provide educational assistance to members.
Requires such assistance to be used by members for expenses incurred in pursuing their first undergraduate, master's, vocational, or technical degree.

topic.name: U.S. Trade and Tariff Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures related to tariffs, import restrictions, and trade regulations affecting U.S. industries and international trade.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend through 2027 the production certificate program
refund of duties paid on such articles imported into the United States.
Document 2: prohibits, for a specified period, the importation of certain iron and steel articles
into the customs territory of the United States.
Document 3: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 regarding the imposition of countervailing duties on imports
merchandise from a nonmarket country, unless the administering authority cannot identify and measure subsidies.

topic.name: International Relations and Development
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing international relations and development through trade, cultural exchange, and volunteerism.

Document 1: Amends the Caribbean Basin Economic Recovery Act to extend the date for Haiti's qualification for preferential trade provisions.
Focus on political, economic, social, and security requirements for Haiti.
Document 2: Authorizes grants to promote understanding of the U.S. through documentary films.
Supports cultural exchange and global perspectives.
Document 3: Establishes a Global Service Fellowship Program to promote international volunteering.
Aims to build bridges across cultures and address human needs.

topic.name: Health Care Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to health care coverage and provisions for specific populations, particularly children and individuals affected by mandatory coverage requirements.

Document 1: Enhancing Health Care Coverage for Children Act
revise, reauthorize, and extend the SCHIP program
Document 2: End the Mandate Act of 2010
repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses amendments to various financial acts aimed at enhancing oversight and accountability in federal financial assistance and housing finance.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Housing Enterprises Financial Safety and Soundness Act of 1992
requires the use of 100% of certain Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac allocations to reimburse the Treasury
Document 2: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008
Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to provide ongoing updates of the status of the use of funds distributed under EESA
Document 3: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965
to increase the amount of the outstanding principal and interest on capital financing bonds for historically Black colleges and universities

topic.name: Equity in Education and Services
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at ensuring equitable access to educational opportunities and services for all individuals, particularly focusing on gender, race, and other demographic factors.

Document 1: ensure that gender specific services are continuously available
provide such services based on the standards of the VA
Document 2: requires each institution of higher education to submit an annual report
provides certain additional disclosures regarding admissions process
Document 3: require state and local educational agencies to conduct an annual equity gap assessment
create plans on how to reduce the gap

topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: A collection of federal legislative acts addressing various policy initiatives and local governance issues.

Document 1: Establishes in the executive branch a Commission on Evidence-Based Policymaking.
Conducts a comprehensive study of the data inventory, data infrastructure, and statistical protocols related to federal policymaking.
Document 2: Promote recreational opportunities for youth and commemorate women's history in the city of Tulare, California.
The United States authorizes the conveyance of, and relinquishes its reversionary interest in, specified Tulare Railroad Proposed Parcels.
Document 3: Repeals the law stating: U.S. coins and currency are legal tender for all debts, public charges, taxes and dues.
Foreign gold or silver coins are not legal tender for debts.

topic.name: Agricultural Product Promotion and Taxation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the promotion of agricultural products and the taxation implications for producers, particularly in the context of orange juice and distilled spirits.

Document 1: Establishes within the Treasury the Orange Juice Promotion and Production Improvements Trust Fund
to provide grants to U.S. entities for the generic promotion of orange juice in the U.S. market.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to exclude from determination of the production period for distilled spirits
to determine whether a taxpayer can expense, rather than capitalize, interest costs paid or incurred during the production period.

topic.name: North Korean Child Adoption and U.S. Policy
topic.description: Documents related to U.S. legislative efforts to facilitate the adoption of North Korean children and diplomatic interactions with Taiwan.

Document 1: North Korean Refugee Adoption Act of 2010
facilitating the adoption of North Korean children by U.S. citizens.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of State to provide briefings to Congress
discussions conducted between any executive branch agency and the government of Taiwan.
Document 3: North Korean Refugee Adoption Act of 2011
develop a comprehensive strategy for facilitating the adoption of North Korean children by U.S. citizens.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing public health crises and neglected diseases in the United States.

Document 1: Neglected diseases of poverty, including neglected parasitic diseases
Requires the report to provide the information necessary to guide future health policy
Document 2: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act
EPA must waive state NPDES permit requirements for public health pesticide applications
Document 3: Prohibit any individual from entering the United States for 30 days following the individual's presence in a country experiencing an Ebola outbreak
Ensure individuals are certified by a medical professional as testing negative for Ebola

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments addressing federal codes and regulations in the United States.

Document 1: This bill amends the federal criminal code by allowing a governmental entity to require providers of electronic communication services to disclose the contents of communications.
The bill allows a governmental entity to obtain those communications only if a court finds that the governmental entity has taken all reasonable steps.
Document 2: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to apply payroll and self-employment taxes to remuneration up to the Social Security contribution and benefit base.
Document 3: Amends the Gulf of Mexico Energy Security Act of 2006 to direct the Secretary of the Treasury to deposit 87.5% of qualified outer Continental Shelf (OCS) revenues into the general fund of the Treasury.

topic.name: Environmental Safety Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at ensuring safety and compliance in environmental practices, particularly related to public health and hazardous materials management.

Document 1: Authorize the Secretary of Commerce to provide grants to assist the state for cimex lectularius, commonly known as the bed bug.
Educate lodging proprietors and staff about prevention and eradication of bed bugs.
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration (PHMSA) to review the adequacy of PHMSA regulations.
Requires the Administrator to establish a database of all pipeline water crossings in the United States.
Document 3: Requires the operator of a drilling and production operation to submit annually a specified certification by the operator's chief executive officer.
Subjects the chief executive officer to a civil penalty for non-compliance.

topic.name: Social Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at reforming social welfare programs and federal agency transparency.

Document 1: Amends the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996
repeal the denial to drug felons of eligibility for benefits under the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families (TANF) program.
Document 2: Federal Agency Transparency Act
requires each federal agency to publish on its official website a statement of all funds received and spent during such period.
Document 3: Amends civil service law concerning federal workers' compensation
authorize the use of clinical social workers to conduct evaluations to determine work-related emotional and mental illnesses.

topic.name: Regulatory Frameworks in Sports and Energy
topic.description: This topic encompasses the regulatory measures and legislative actions concerning energy projects and professional combat sports, focusing on licensing, safety, and operational guidelines.

extend the license period to 70 years upon licensee request.
Document 2: Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury and the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System from proposing regulations
regulation pursuant to the prohibition against acceptance of a financial instrument for unlawful internet gambling.
Document 3: amends the Professional Boxing Safety Act of 1996
establish definitions for 'fighter,' 'combat sport competition,' and 'mixed martial arts'

topic.name: Employment Support for Veterans
topic.description: The documents discuss federal initiatives aimed at enhancing employment opportunities for military personnel and older adults through partnerships with nonprofit organizations.

Document 1: federal government should support private sector employment initiatives for military personnel
authorize the Chief of the National Guard Bureau to award grants to nonprofit organizations
Document 2: federal government should support private sector employment initiatives for military personnel
authorize the Chief of the National Guard Bureau to award grants to nonprofit organizations

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Education and Telecommunications
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures aimed at regulating education funding and telecommunications infrastructure.

Document 1: Korea Security Partnership Act - Deems the Republic of Korea to be a 'favorable treatment country' eligible for the export license exception for encryption commodities, software and technology under the Export Administration Regulations.
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to repeal provisions relating to assistance for the planning and construction of public telecommunications facilities.
Document 3: Foreign Medical School Accountability Fairness Act of 2014 - Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to eliminate the exemption of certain foreign medical schools from the prohibition on foreign medical schools participating in the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan program.

topic.name: Passport and Identity Verification Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and policies regarding passport issuance, identity verification for banking, and measures related to national security and foreign terrorist organizations.

Document 1: Authorizes a financial institution to accept only a Social Security number, passport number, or alien identification card number for identity verification.
Prohibits acceptance of identification documents issued by a foreign government, other than an unexpired passport.
Document 2: Amends the Passport Act of 1926 to authorize the Department of State to refuse to issue a passport to individuals who have aided foreign terrorist organizations.
Authorizes the Department to revoke a passport previously issued to such individuals.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of State to establish passport issuance agencies within 50 miles of major international border crossings.
Defines 'major international border crossing' based on vehicle crossing volume.

topic.name: Water and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to water resource management and environmental regulations in the context of sustainability and resource reclamation.

Mojave Water Agency's Integrated Regional Water Management Plan.
Document 2: Amends the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act of 1977
Abandoned Mine Reclamation Fund for states having an approved abandoned mine reclamation program.

forest biomass emissions do not increase overall carbon accumulations in the atmosphere.

topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing government oversight, advocacy, and support for specific groups and programs.

assist in making claims for benefits for Department of Energy (DOE) contractor employees
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense to review each covered contract
determine if the contract has been the subject of fraud or any other contracting impropriety
Document 3: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965
making substantial contributions to providing Black Americans with graduate education opportunities

topic.name: Child Welfare and Literacy Programs
topic.description: Legislative initiatives aimed at improving child welfare through literacy enhancement and safety measures.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to nonprofit organizations for the implementation of Pediatric Early Literacy Programs
health care providers encourage parents to read aloud to their children
Document 2: establish policies and procedures for a program for national criminal history background checks for child-serving organizations
assist such organizations in obtaining access to nationwide background checks
Document 3: provide eligible organizations and individuals expedited access to federal land to conduct good Samaritan search-and-recovery missions
develop search-and-recovery focused partnerships with search-and-recovery organizations

topic.name: Government Directives and Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various directives and legislative actions related to the establishment of services, project extensions, and appointments within government agencies.

Document 1: establish a national cemetery in eastern Nebraska to serve the needs of veterans
veterans and their families in eastern Nebraska and western Iowa.
Document 2: extend for three years after enactment of this Act the time period
licensee is required to commence the construction of project works.
Document 3: Amends the National Security Agency Act of 1959
require the Director of the National Security Agency (NSA) to be appointed by the President.

topic.name: Transportation Infrastructure and Economic Impact
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving transportation infrastructure and assessing the economic effects of border wait times on trade and mobility.

Document 1: Amends the Transportation Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act (TIFIA) to make eligible for TIFIA funding any projects to improve or construct public infrastructure located within one-half mile of the station area of a fixed-guideway transit facility.
improvements to mobility and parking, rehabilitation or construction of streets, transit stations, structured parking, or walkways and bikeways.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Transportation to study and report to Congress on the traffic patterns of commercial and passenger vehicles that enter and exit U.S. international land ports of entry along the northern and southern borders.
Directs the Secretary of Commerce to study and report to Congress on the negative economic impact, if any, on the United States of wait times at such ports of entry.

topic.name: U.S. Territories and Veterans Affairs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and policies related to the management of veterans' affairs and healthcare services in U.S. territories and insular areas.

Document 1: Caring for the Fallen Act - Makes the American Battle Monuments Commission responsible for Clark Veterans Cemetery in the Philippines, including ensuring its ongoing maintenance.
Document 2: Includes Puerto Rico and Guam within the coverage area of TRICARE Prime (a Department of Defense [DOD] managed health care program).
Document 3: Insular Areas Energy Act of 2007 - Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to authorize the Secretary of Energy to make loan guarantees for target ocean thermal energy conversion systems projects.

topic.name: Weather and Climate Monitoring
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at improving the monitoring, forecasting, and management of weather-related phenomena and climate impacts.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of NOAA to carry out a program for an improved ocean surface winds vector satellite
to improve forecasting for hurricanes, coastal winds and storm surge, and other weather-related disasters
Document 2: Rescinds an equal amount of unobligated balances available for NOAA, for operations, research, and facilities
Document 3: the Under Secretary of Commerce for Oceans and Atmosphere shall continue to support the National Integrated Drought Information System (NIDIS) Program
to better inform and provide for more timely decisionmaking to reduce drought related impacts and costs

topic.name: Government Studies and Reporting
topic.description: The documents focus on directives for government agencies to conduct studies and report findings on various topics related to military identification, intelligence contractor usage, and carbon content labeling.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Defense to study and report to Congress on the feasibility of using a distinct military identification number
to identify each member of the Armed Forces.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to study the extent to which contractors are used in the conduct of intelligence activities
to submit a report containing the results of the study and a plan for reducing the number of intelligence community contractors.
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to study and report to Congress on the feasibility of establishing a national program for measuring, reporting, publicly disclosing, and labeling products or materials sold in the United States for their carbon content
Requires the Administrator, upon concluding the study, to establish a national product carbon disclosure program.

topic.name: Government Oversight and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at ensuring proper oversight and operational continuity within government agencies and financial institutions.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Agriculture from closing or relocating any Farm Service Agency (FSA) county or field office until at least one year after enactment of an omnibus law.
continuation of agricultural programs for fiscal years after 2007.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to establish, maintain, and modify as necessary procedures for ensuring the effective coordination of outreach activities.
Authorizes the Secretary to make grants to state or county veterans agencies for state and local outreach services.
Document 3: Requires each financial institution to publish, within 30 days after the end of each fiscal quarter, a statement of its financial condition.
Requires such financial statements to include a written verification of the accuracy of the statement, under oath or by affirmation.

topic.name: Financial Oversight and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing oversight and regulation of financial entities and programs to ensure accountability and prevent excessive risk-taking.

require the Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to prohibit any golden parachute payment
Document 2: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008
bring under the oversight authority of the Comptroller General any entity participating in the Troubled Asset Relief Program

topic.name: Health and Cybersecurity Legislation

disclose to Congress health insurance premium increase information
Document 2: applicant for benefits under title XXI
satisfactory documentation providing both citizenship and identity
Document 3: Botnet Prevention Act of 2016
expand prohibited conduct and increase criminal penalties with respect to cybercrime

topic.name: Government Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at regulating government operations, education standards, and citizenship processes.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to issue guidance to prohibit the download, installation, or use by government employees and contractors of open-network peer-to-peer file sharing software.
Requires the Director to develop a procedure for receiving requests from heads or chief information officers of agencies for approval for use by government employees and contractors.
Document 2: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to suspend new identifications of schools or local educational agencies as needing restructuring for failing to make adequate yearly progress.
Lifts such suspension on the earlier of the last day of the 2012-2013 school year or the reauthorization of the ESEA.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to make funds available annually to the Director of U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services for public ceremonies for administering oaths of allegiance.
Specifies ceremony fund limits ($5,000) and permitted fund uses.

topic.name: Legislative Policy Analysis

cancellation notice must include information under specified circumstances
Document 2: Homeland Security Drone Assessment and Analysis Act directs DHS to research unmanned aircraft
requires DHS to disseminate information regarding attacks by such aircraft
Document 3: Community Bank Preservation Act amends the Federal Reserve Act
requires Board of Governors to include members with community bank experience

topic.name: Chemical Safety and Health Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on health and safety regulations related to chemical substances and their management in various contexts.

require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to publish the decision to approve or deny an application for a waiver

members of the Armed Forces may use such agents under the general rules of engagement

Child Care and Development Block Grant Program

monitoring the water quality and living resources of the Gulf ecosystem
Document 3: develop and implement an ongoing and sustainable national strategy for identifying and evaluating the health status
health care needs of Native Hawaiians and Other Pacific Islanders

topic.name: Mortgage Fraud Legislation

employment of 30 additional agents of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations to the Department of Justice for FY2009-FY2013
employment of 30 additional agents of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)

topic.name: Educational Accountability and Safety Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing accountability and safety in educational settings, particularly regarding athletic programs and school safety policies for field trips.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to direct coeducational elementary and secondary schools to report annually certain information on equality in their school athletic programs.
Directs the Commissioner to ensure that such data are posted on the Department of Education's Web site.
Document 2: Requires each local educational agency (LEA) to develop and make publicly available a written school safety policy for off-premises, overnight field trips.
Document 3: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to direct coeducational elementary and secondary schools to report annually certain information on equality in their school athletic programs.
Directs the Commissioner to ensure that such data are posted on the Department of Education's Web site.

topic.name: Government Agency Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses the regulations and requirements for federal agencies regarding conference reporting, anti-doping measures, and healthcare initiatives in underserved areas.

Document 1: Requires the head of a federal agency who is hosting a conference or sending an agency employee to a conference to submit to Congress a report.
Defines 'conference' as a meeting, retreat, seminar, symposium, training, or other event that involves travel for 50 or more attendees.
Document 2: Amends the Office of National Drug Control Policy Reauthorization Act of 2006 to authorize appropriations for the U.S. Anti-Doping Agency.
Require that the Agency be recognized worldwide as the independent national anti-doping organization for the United States.
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a program to make grants to accredited schools of allopathic or osteopathic medicine.
Requires the Secretary to convene a Rural Training Program Symposium to develop best practices for such schools.

topic.name: Federal Regulation and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses the legislative framework and oversight mechanisms related to federal regulations, economic development, and the use of technology in military and civilian sectors.

Document 3: Amends the Public Works and Economic Development Act of 1965 to prohibit a recipient of an economic adjustment grant from making, but permits a venture development organization to make, a grant, equity investment, or convertible loan to a private for-profit entity.
Defines a 'venture development organization' as a public entity or private, nonprofit entity that provides to an early-stage technology business, to promote the growth of that business.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Accountability
topic.description: The documents discuss federal actions related to land management and budgetary accountability within federal agencies.

Document 1: Amends the Coastal Barrier Resources Act to remove areas from the Coastal Barrier Resources System in Florida.
Discusses federal land management regarding coastal areas.
Document 2: Establishes the Commission on Congressional Budgetary Accountability and Review of Federal Agencies.
Requires assessment of agency programs for effectiveness and accountability.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey federally owned land in Florida.
Involves federal land transfer to local government.

topic.name: Food Safety and Nutrition Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at ensuring food safety, improving nutrition, and supporting educational pathways in agriculture and health sectors.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of funds appropriated to the Department of Agriculture to approve or contract with any person or entity to approve for human consumption any cow, sheep, pig, goat, or horse, mule, or other equine that does not stand and walk unassisted at any time while the animal is alive at a slaughtering, packing, meat-canning, rendering, or similar establishment.
Document 2: Directs the President to call the White House Conference on Food and Nutrition to be convened in order to make fundamental policy recommendations on ways to end hunger and to improve nutrition in the United States.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments to federal laws related to education funding, intelligence surveillance, and the use of federal funds.

Document 1: Amends part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
reserves 0.5% of those funds for outlying areas and 0.75% for Indian education.
Document 2: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978
requires the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to include a statement that the information sought is relevant to an authorized investigation.

Document 3: Prohibits the use of federal funds for a project or program named for an individual currently serving as a Member, Delegate, Resident Commissioner, or Senator of the U.S. Congress.

topic.name: Maritime and Fisheries Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal laws and reforms related to maritime operations, fishing permits, and habitat protection in marine environments.

Document 1: revises federal shipping laws governing maritime liability
prohibit maritime liens from being imposed on state or federal fishing permits
Document 2: directs the Secretary of the Interior to assess each offshore oil and gas platform
prohibits the removal of any such platforms until the Secretary has completed each assessment
Document 3: amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Reauthorization Act
requires the Secretary to perform a stock survey and stock assessment of each of the overfished stocks in the fishery

topic.name: Classified Information Procedures

Document 2: amends the Classified Information Procedures Act

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Public Benefit
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at promoting conservation, health services, and clean energy initiatives.

Document 1: Amends the Neotropical Migratory Bird Conservation Act
authorize the appropriation to the Neotropical Migratory Bird Conservation Fund
Document 2: Geriatrics Loan Forgiveness Act of 2009
deem each year an individual is enrolled in a geriatric training program as a year of obligated service
Document 3: Clean Energy Partnership Act of 2007
provide grants to local educational agencies covering up to 50% of their costs in implementing clean energy projects

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving educational systems and student support in both primary and higher education.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
Allows schools to be given credit for performing well on measures other than test scores
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to revise specified regulations regarding student loan repayment deferment
Revises such regulations to make such deferment available to borrowers in postgraduate medical or dental internship
Document 3: Amends the Public Health Service Act to reauthorize appropriations for National Health Service Corps Scholarship and Loan Repayment programs
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to reserve a portion of such funds for scholarships to individuals enrolled in a course of study in medicine or osteopathic medicine

topic.name: Veterans and Military Health Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on healthcare and support for military personnel and veterans.

Document 1: Military Equitable Reimbursement Act of 2013
Department of Defense (DOD) reimbursement for media producer assistance.
Document 2: Expedited Hiring for VA Trained Psychiatrists Act of 2016
Appointment of psychiatrists in Veterans Health Administration without regard to civil service laws.
Document 3: Novel Device Regulatory Relief Act of 2011
Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act for device classification.

topic.name: Maritime Shipping Regulations
topic.description: The topic focuses on legislative amendments and regulations concerning maritime shipping practices, particularly related to coastwise endorsement and customs requirements for vessels operating between U.S. ports and territories like Puerto Rico.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to waive the requirement that Canadian vessels report immediately to the nearest customs facility upon arrival at any U.S. port or place or the Virgin Islands
if, in fact, they do not anchor or dock at any harbor within the U.S. customs territory.
Document 2: Amends federal shipping law to revise coastwise endorsement requirements for certain vessels that transport liquified natural gas or liquified petroleum gas to Puerto Rico from other U.S. ports.

Exempts from coastwise endorsement requirements self-propelled vessels that transport bulk cargo, including liquified natural gas or liquified petroleum bulk cargo, to Puerto Rico from another U.S. port or from Puerto Rico to another U.S. port.

topic.name: Legislative Initiatives for Community Support

Document 3: require a Congressional Budget Office analysis of public bills

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing transparency and accountability within federal agencies and programs.

Document 1: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to require the head of an element of the intelligence community... to report such request to the Attorney General and the congressional intelligence committees.
Revises the definition of 'covert agent' for purposes of such Act.
Document 2: Directs the Inspector General (IG) of the Federal Housing Finance Agency to submit quarterly reports to Congress on the IG's activities...
on the Federal National Mortgage Association (Fannie Mae) and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (Freddie Mac) and their activities and condition while they are in conservatorship status.
Document 3: Authorizes a faculty member of a military service academy... to secure federal copyright protection for a scholarly work prepared as part of that person's official duties.
Requires the faculty member to transfer such copyright to the owner or publisher of the medium for which the work will be published.

topic.name: Federal Health and Social Services Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on federal legislation and initiatives related to health, social services, and the promotion of specific demographic groups.

Document 1: Directs the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to convey to the Yukon Kuskokwim Health Corporation in Bethel, Alaska, all U.S. interest in specified property for use in connection with health and social services programs.
Shields the Corporation from liability for any soil, surface water, groundwater, or other contamination resulting from the disposal, release, or presence of environmental contamination.
Document 2: Establishes the Presidential Commission on Women to review the status of women nationwide and the role of the federal government in aid to, and the promotion of, women.

topic.name: Economic Development and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing economic development through regulatory frameworks and community support initiatives.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to enter into an agreement with Northwestern State University
providing additional space for use by the National Center for Preservation Technology and Training
Document 2: Sets forth general requirements for the federal regulatory system
promote predictability in the regulatory process
Document 3: authorize the use of grants for economic adjustment for activities identified by communities
increase the federal share for a grant to up to 100% of the cost of a project

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Use
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, transfer, and use of federal lands, including provisions for specific groups and activities such as filming and park funding.

Document 1: Transfers certain land in Riverside and San Diego counties, California, from the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to the United States to be held in trust for the Pechanga Band of Luiseno Mission Indians.
Prohibits such transfer from including the 12.82 acres of lands more or less.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to require a permit and assess an annual fee of $200 for commercial filming activities on federal land.
Bars the Secretary from prohibiting the use of cameras or related equipment used for commercial filming activities.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer specified funds previously appropriated to the National Park Service to counties within the Great Smoky Mountains National Park.
